labels.name: get_report_markdown
labels.description: Fetch a finished report as compact, agent-ready Markdown: health, a prioritized fix plan with remediation, and issues collapsed to one row per code (code | severity | count | example URLs) — small enough to keep in context on a real site. Set full=true for the complete human report (large). Use get_

labels.name: get_issue_detail
labels.description: Drill into one issue code: every affected URL plus step-by-step remediation (what to change, why, and when it's a false alarm). Call this after get_report_markdown to expand a code from the collapsed table before applying fixes.

labels.name: diff_scan
labels.description: Diff a scan against the previous scan of the same domain: health movement plus the issue codes that were fixed or newly appeared/regressed. Use this when auditing in a loop to verify fixes landed and nothing regressed.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: scan_url, get_report, get_report_markdown, get_issue_detail · Public sink: scan_url.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: scan_url, get_report, get_report_markdown, get_issue_detail · Public sink: scan_url

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
